title: sciodaphyllum jefense

constituents: Sciodaphyllum jefense has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and cancer. parts: Sciodaphyllum jefense has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and bark. The leaves of the Sciodaphyllum jefense plant are rich in flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant contain alkaloids, such as sciodaphyllumine, which has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The bark of the Sciodaphyllum jefense plant is also rich in tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat fever and other inflammatory conditions.
side_effects: Sciodaphyllum jefense can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems due to its toxic sap. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or seeds can lead to symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, as well as increased heart rate and blood pressure.
